title: Untouched-Fold Logit Blend With Beta or Venn-Abers Calibration
summary: Prior work already pointed toward specialist blends; public calibration literature suggests Beta calibration and Venn-Abers are stronger post-hoc options than plain Platt in many binary settings.
rationale: Prior work already pointed toward specialist blends; public calibration literature suggests Beta calibration and Venn-Abers are stronger post-hoc options than plain Platt in many binary settings. Inference: the gain is likeliest after stacking heterogeneous specialists, not from calibrating one model alone.
implementation_hint: Generate OOF predictions from the baseline classifier, ranker, monotone specialist, and latent-wear specialist; fit a simple blender on one grouped OOF layer, then reserve a final untouched grouped fold only for Beta or Venn-Abers calibration.
expected_effect: Mostly a stability play: cleaner probability scale for ensemble combination and less public-LB drift from overconfident rare positives.
risk: Calibration alone will not improve AUC much and can waste data if the held-out calibration fold is too small.
